title: Lär av historien
summary_md:
# Lär av historien

**Feed**: aktiepodden  
**Published**: 2026-05-15  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A special history-focused episode of Aktiepodden with economic journalist Torun Nilsson discussing his new book on 50 years of Swedish financial-market history — the transformation from one of the world's most regulated markets to a vibrant equity culture, told through deregulation, the 1980s boom, the early-90s banking crisis, the late-90s tech/telecom boom and bust, 2008, and the rise of broad retail ownership via funds and ISK. No actionable stock calls in the main segment; the appended sponsored interview with Byggmästaren CEO Thomas Bergström highlights bullish views on core holdings Safelife (defibrillators) and Green Landscaping. Overall tone is optimistic about the long-run value of equity ownership in Sweden.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 GREEN — Green Landscaping | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Sponsored interview; CEO of part-owner Byggmästaren is bullish on structural growth but flags Sweden/Norway turnaround in progress.

#### Reasoning

Byggmästaren CEO frames Green as Northern Europe's largest grounds-care operator riding structural tailwinds (urbanisation, safe/green cities, underinvested infrastructure). Sweden challenges turned up in the latest report; Norway issues being worked on actively. Top priority is returning the company to growth. Positive but promotional and turnaround-dependent, hence low conviction.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Norwegian operations fail to stabilise
- if Swedish growth recovery reverses
- if structural urbanisation demand weakens

> *"Zoomar jag ut så är Green Norra Europas största aktör inom utemiljö, skötsel och finplanering. Det är en marknad som också strukturellt växer"*

---

### 🟠 BMAH — Byggmästare Anders J Ahlström Holding | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Sponsored segment; CEO reports modest NAV growth and active value-building in core holdings amid limited Q1 activity.

#### Reasoning

CEO Thomas Bergström reports NAV up 2% vs year-end and vs a year ago, with limited Q1 deal activity beyond the GT Media completion. Strategy centres on backing exceptional entrepreneurs with expansion capital in scalable core holdings (Safelife, Green Landscaping). Promotional context and flat-ish NAV growth keep this a low-conviction watch rather than a buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if NAV growth stalls or reverses
- if core holdings underperform
- if the discount to NAV widens persistently

> *"Vi hade en rapport där vi ökade substansen i första kvartalet med 2% jämfört med årsskiftet och faktiskt 2% jämfört med för ett år sedan."*

---
